data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Thursday: NKE, RL, INTU and WDAY
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures on Thursday were little changed, as market participants kept their focus on the passing of a major tax bill. Here are some stocks to watch on Thursday: 

  • Shares of Nike (NYSE:NKE) were +1.6% in pre-market trading. According to media reports, the Dow 30 component is planning to sell its products directly on Amazon (AMZN) in the U.S. for the first time since 2019. The world's largest shoe company has been struggling with falling sales and lower demand and has unveiled a turnaround plan, and the return to the e-commerce retailer's website is part of that strategy. Nike's (NYSE:NKE) products are currently sold by independent merchants on Amazon (AMZN).
  • Ralph Lauren (NYSE:RL) stock was -4% ahead of the opening bell. The iconic fashion firm delivered a FQ4 2025 top- and bottom-line beat. Ralph Lauren's (NYSE:RL) quarterly global direct-to-consumer comparable store sales climbed 13%, and 10% for the full fiscal year 2025. For fiscal 2026, the company sees revenue growth of low-single digits on a constant currency basis, "reflecting caution on the global operating environment."
  • Shares of Intuit (NASDAQ:INTU) will get some attention, ahead of the financial and accounting software developer's FQ3 2025 results after the closing bell. Wall Street expects the Mountain View, Calif.-based firm to earn $10.91 per share on revenue of $7.56B. Intuit (NASDAQ:INTU) owns several software products such as tax preparation tool TurboTax, bookkeeper QuickBooks, and online credit score service Credit Karma.
  • Workday (NASDAQ:WDAY) stock will also get some eyes, ahead of the enterprise management cloud software firm's FQ1 2026 report after hours. Wall Street expects Pleasanton, Calif.-based WDAY to earn $2.01 per share on revenue of $2.22B. The top-line figure is seen rising more than 15% Y/Y, while the bottom-line number is anticipated to grow nearly 12%. Workday (NASDAQ:WDAY) provides a platform that helps organizations manage money, people and agents.

data.title: J Capital says it's short Marqeta
data.description:
• J Capital Research said on Thursday it's short the card issuing platform Marqeta (NASDAQ:MQ).
  • "An FBI investigation could prompt MQ to lose up to 70% of its business. Future revenue looks weak," said J Capital Research in a report.
  • MQ shares were trading -5.37% pre-market at $4.93.

More on Marqeta

    • Marqeta, Inc. 2025 Q1 - Results - Earnings Call Presentation
    • Marqeta, Inc. (MQ) Q1 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
    • Marqeta: High Risk, Higher Reward
    • Marqeta upgraded at J.P. Morgan on progress made since March 2023 downgrade
    • Marqeta outlines 2025 revenue growth of 13%-15% amid platform expansions and new partnerships

data.title: CBAK Energy Technology announces $20M share buyback program
data.description:
• CBAK Energy Technology (NASDAQ:CBAT) approved a share repurchase program with authorization to purchase up to $20M of shares of its common stock. 
  • The share repurchase program is designed to regain compliance with Nasdaq’s minimum bid price requirement. 
  • This program terminates on May 20, 2026.
  • CBAT shares fall 4% premarket on Thursady. 
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Freight Technologies announces 1-for-4 reverse stock split; shares down
data.description:
• Freight Technologies (NASDAQ:FRGT) approved a one-for-four reverse share splits of its ordinary shares.
  • The ordinary shares will begin trading on a split adjusted basis on May 27, 2025.
  • The number of outstanding ordinary shares will be reduced from 9.15M to 2.29M.
  • The reverse split will not affect the number of total authorized ordinary shares.
  • FRGT shares down over 12% premarket on Thursday. 
  • Source: Press release

data.title: BP seeks buyers for Castrol lubricants unit to reach $20B divestment goal - Reuters
data.description:
BP (NYSE:BP) has started a sale process for its Castrol lubricants business and has hired Goldman Sachs to help it sell the unit, Reuters reported Thursday, marking a key step in the company's divestment plan to boost its share price.

BP (NYSE:BP) said in February that it was reviewing Castrol as part of a plan to raise $20B by 2027 through asset sales to lower its debt, and Reuters reported in March that Saudi Aramco was one of the parties weighing a potential bid for the unit.

A sale could raise $8B-$10B based on Castrol's projected core earnings of $1B for 2024, according to the report.

As part of its broader asset sale plan, BP (BP) also is looking to sell its Gelsenkirchen refinery and its mobility and convenience businesses in Austria, as well as the sale of a 50% stake in Lightsource bp, with bidders due to be short-listed in July.

Investors pressured BP (BP) to improve profitability after its shares have lost ~25% of their value in the last 12 months, more than double the drop suffered by rival Shell.

data.title: Metallis launches up to $1.35M private placement of units
data.description:
Minerals exploration company Metallis Resources (OTCQB:MTLFF) (TSXV:MTS:CA) announced on Thursday an up to $1.35M non-brokered private placement of up to 8.437M units priced at $0.16 per unit.

Each unit consists of one share and one non-transferable share purchase warrant. The warrant is exercisable into one share at a price of $0.26 per share for a 3-year period.

The proceeds will be used for field exploration work at the company's Greyhound and Kirkham Properties and for general corporate purposes.

data.title: Mobile Infrastructure uplists to Nasdaq
data.description:
• Mobile Infrastructure (NYSE:BEEP) will commence trading on Nasdaq under its current ticker symbol “BEEP” on Friday, May 23.
  • Mobile Infrastructure’s stock will continue to trade on the NYSE American until the market close on Thursday, May 22, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release
